title: The Multiplier — Mentorship Program Design
prerequisites: Advanced Offensive Track - Mastery Tier Access
learning_objectives: Program architecture design with tiered advancement
Learning path development for diverse backgrounds
Training material creation and instructional design
scenario: This Mastery mission requires professional-grade execution in African cyber talent development ecosystem. Challenge: Design comprehensive mentorship and training program to develop next generation of African red teamers. Relevance: OCH mission includes building African cyber capacity; Mastery graduates lead mentorship initiatives

subtasks.detailed_guidance:

    6. Prepare materials for senior mentor evaluation

resources.title: Mission Overview: The Multiplier — Mentorship Program Design

reflection_prompt: What would an ideal mentorship program look like for African red team candidates? How would you structure learning paths for people from diverse technical backgrounds?

quiz.question: When designing a mentorship program for African red team candidates, why is tiered advancement with clear milestones important?
options: Tiered advancement is unnecessary if mentors are skilled
Clear milestones provide candidates with measurable progress, accountability, and structured progression—essential for sustainable talent development in emerging African cybersecurity ecosystem
All candidates progress at the same rate regardless of capability
Mentorship programs should have no structure
quiz.explanation: Tiered advancement creates clear pathways for African talent development. Measurable milestones provide candidates with progress visibility, enable mentors to identify gaps, and allow organizations to graduate candidates at different paces based on demonstrated capability.

quiz.question: In your annual community contribution plan, what distinguishes high-impact contributions from token engagement?
options: Impact and engagement are the same
High-impact contributions directly develop African cyber talent (mentoring individuals, creating training materials, speaking at regional conferences) versus generic activities
Community contribution is only for marketing purposes
Individual impact is impossible to measure
quiz.explanation: Meaningful mentorship requires specific commitments: mentoring named individuals with documented progress, creating training materials used by others, speaking at African cybersecurity conferences, and open-source contributions. These are measurable and impactful versus generic 'giving back.'

portfolio_artifacts: Evidence of technical excellence and strategic thinking
mentor_notes:
This Mastery mission requires senior mentor review. Candidates must demonstrate:
- Professional-grade execution and documentation
- Deep understanding of African organizational and threat contexts
- Strategic thinking beyond tactical execution
- Readiness for commercial red team operations
- Leadership potential within OCH and broader community

Successful completion positions candidate for Mastery portfolio and professional practice.
